baseline_dirty_snapshot:
M commands/INDEX.md
 M commands/dev.md
 M docs/dev/INDEX.md
 M docs/dev/specs/INDEX.md
 M docs/dev/specs/spec-20260520-221059.md
 M hooks/INDEX.md
 M hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh
 M tests/generated/INDEX.md
 M tests/generated/manifest.json

tasks_completed.description: Extended outer guard trigger in pretool-bash-safety.sh (M1+M2)
tasks_completed.changes: Added third OR condition '|| echo "$COMMAND" | grep -qF 'write-bulk-commit-sentinel.py'' to Layer 1.F outer guard trigger at line ~669. This captures M1 (absolute path invocation) and M2 (python3 -c with script name literal), which were previously not triggering the outer guard because they contain no /tmp/claude-bulk-* path literals.
tasks_completed.rationale: Root cause: outer guard only matched on /tmp/ path patterns, missing the script name itself as an attack surface

tasks_completed.description: Added M5 bash allowlist for canonical venv-activate form
tasks_completed.changes: Added M5 bash [[ =~ ]] allowlist check BEFORE the Python compound-detection helper. Regex: '^source[[:space:]]+venv/bin/activate[[:space:]]*&&[[:space:]]*python3?[[:space:]]+/root/\.claude/scripts/write-bulk-commit-sentinel\.py$'. If the canonical form matches exactly (no extra args), exits 0. Uses [[ =~ ]] not echo|grep to prevent newline-injection bypass.
tasks_completed.rationale: Without M5, the new trigger would block the legitimate /commit --bulk Step 5 command. The regex is end-anchored ($) so --sid FORGED variants don't match and fall through to DENY.

tasks_completed.description: Added DO NOT items 13 and 14 to agents/changelog-analyst.md (M3+M4)
tasks_completed.changes: Item 13: Never use auto-bulk: commit message prefix when BULK=false. Item 14: Never create/cause-creation of /tmp/claude-bulk-commit-sentinel-*.json by any mechanism (covers arch-2b which is unblockable at hook layer).
tasks_completed.rationale: arch-2b (fully dynamic construction with no script name literal) cannot be blocked by hook pattern matching; agent-level prohibition is the only defense layer available

tasks_completed.description: Implemented real test logic in 6 pre-generated test stubs (AC1-AC6)
tasks_completed.changes: Replaced placeholder bodies with real subprocess-based hook invocations (AC1-AC3), content-scan tests (AC4-AC5), and pytest delegation test (AC6). All 6 pass.
tasks_completed.rationale: Test stubs were placeholders; real implementations verify each acceptance criterion at the function level

tasks_completed.description: Added AC7 and AC7b committed test files (Codex Finding 3 — in_scope_real_bug)
tasks_completed.changes: AC7: canonical venv-activate form (no extra args) exits 0. AC7b: venv-activate form with --sid FORGED exits 2 with bulk-commit-auth-flag-write token. M5 is the most sensitive change; these tests guard the exact regex boundary.
tasks_completed.rationale: Codex found AC7/AC7b were only tested in /tmp/test_hook_direct.py (ephemeral). Committed test files are required for CI regression guarding.

git_rationale.root_cause_commit: commits 1238f42+41063a1 (primary fix) — outer guard only matched /tmp/ path literals, not script name itself
git_rationale.why_issue_occurred: Layer 1.F outer guard trigger used grep -qF on /tmp/ path patterns. Absolute-path invocations (M1) and python3 -c invocations containing the script name (M2) contain no /tmp/ literal, so they bypassed the outer guard entirely.
git_rationale.how_fix_addresses_root: Added third OR condition matching the sentinel-writer script name as a substring. M5 allowlist preserves the one legitimate use case (canonical /commit --bulk venv-activate form) using end-anchored bash [[ =~ ]] regex.

diff_stats.justification_for_overage: Lines_added > 20 because 8 test files were created (required by acceptance criteria). Implementation-only changes (M1+M2+M5 in hook + M3+M4 in changelog) are ~20 lines. The remaining 320 lines are test files, which are explicitly required by the BA spec.

dev.qa_notes: Run: python3 -m pytest tests/generated/20260526-052559/ -v. All 8 tests should pass. Key regression guards: AC3 (relative-path bare form still exits 0), AC7 (canonical venv-activate exits 0), AC7b (forged --sid exits 2). Verify DENY token 'bulk-commit-auth-flag-write' appears in AC1 and AC7b output. Verify DO NOT items 13/14 are present in agents/changelog-analyst.md DO NOT section.

tests: AC-03: relative path allowed regression guard (test_ac_03_ac03-relative-path-allowed.py — PASS)
AC-04: DO NOT item 13 (auto-bulk BULK=false prohibition) present (test_ac_04 — PASS)
AC-05: DO NOT item 14 (sentinel writer prohibition) present (test_ac_05 — PASS)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: 3 findings: (1) Relative-path bare-writer still allows sentinel forging via relative path — marked out_of_scope per BA spec constraint AC3 requirement. (2) M5 allowlist available to subagents — marked in_scope_minor; BA spec does not include IS_SUBAGENT gate; noted in recommendations. (3) AC7/AC7b not in committed test files — marked in_scope_real_bug; applied fix by creating committed test files.
codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Applied Finding 3 (in_scope_real_bug): created test_ac_07_ac07-venv-activate-allowed.py and test_ac_07b_ac07b-forged-arg-blocked.py as committed pytest files. Finding 1 (out_of_scope): not applied — BA spec explicitly requires relative-path bare invocation to remain allowed (AC3 regression guard). Finding 2 (in_scope_minor): noted in recommendations.

self_verification.notes: All 8 pytest tests pass (8/8). Hook syntax verified (bash -n pretool-bash-safety.sh passes). Canonical form exits 0, forged form exits 2, absolute path exits 2, python3 -c exits 2, relative path exits 0. DO NOT items 13/14 confirmed present in agents/changelog-analyst.md.

recommendations: Consider adding IS_SUBAGENT=1 gate to M5 allowlist so canonical /commit --bulk venv-activate form is only accessible from human (non-subagent) context. BA spec did not include this requirement but it would close the subagent-can-self-invoke-canonical-form gap (Codex Finding 2).

arch-2b (fully dynamic construction with no script name literal) is permanently unblockable at hook layer; only agent-level prohibition (DO NOT item 14) covers it. Consider adding a runtime audit log for all /tmp/ write operations to detect violations post-hoc.
